act: IV — Scaling laws (mixed: one confirmed point estimate, two falsified laws)
verification_note: Corrected after auditing logs. Two pre-registered laws were RUN and FAILED their own kill gates. Reported honestly.

rotation_budget_law.status: NOT SUPPORTED — but the ORIGINAL falsification test was INVALID; corrected scan is negative with caveats

original_scan_plus_0.183.verdict: INVALID TEST — both regression axes were mis-specified
problems: max_opt_steps was fixed per variant, so every T_frac variant trained on ~32.8M tokens regardless — the T axis measured corpus diversity, not tokens trained
V_eff was implemented as target masking only (ignore_index); the 50K-way softmax partition function was unchanged — the V axis did not vary partition size
regression x-axis used T_corpus (subset length), not T_trained (tokens optimized)
original_scan_plus_0.183.conclusion: The +0.183 result is a consistent measurement of the wrong experiment. It is NOT evidence the law is false.

corrected_scan_minus_0.2525.verdict: PARTIALLY VALID negative — credible but not definitive
improvements: true V_eff-way head (logits over top-V_eff rows of A_out only, V_eff-class cross-entropy)
T_trained scales with corpus (33.4M / 16.7M / 8.3M tokens across T_frac variants)
remaining_issues: V_eff truncation drops tail target positions, so A across V variants is not the same global LM object (A barely moves: 500 -> 444 -> 432 while V²/T drops ~16x)
short-T variants under-identified: V50K_T0.25 has only 50 fit points, R²(1/t)=0.005; V50K_T0.5 R²=0.35
full-V R²(1/t) ~0.74 — fit window may still include fast-learning phase
corrected_scan_minus_0.2525.conclusion: A does not track V²/T with slope ~ +1 under the corrected protocol — credible negative evidence, but the T branch needs fixed fit windows before this is a definitive kill.

audit.honest_status: The simple A_floor ∝ V²/T power law is NOT SUPPORTED by the best available test. The underlying mechanism (A ∝ Σ_tail 1/N_v) was never directly tested.

rotation_budget_law.source: Experiments/Scaling_law.ipynb + scan code v32_zipf_diagnostics.py (SCAN_VARIANTS, _train_scan_variant, _scan_compute_max_steps) + chat audit cursor_transformer_input_and_output_par.md

cstar_phase_transition.hypothesis: C* ≈ H_language + c·(V²/T) with fixed (H_language=2.698, c=0.1263) from ep2
cstar_phase_transition.gate: delta stays within ±0.05 nats across epochs 3–8
cstar_phase_transition.status: FALSIFIED as stated — but the test only kills the fixed-(H,c) ansatz, not an entropy claim
audit_caveats: C* is the OLS intercept of CE = A/t + C* on TRAIN CE — it is not corpus entropy (WT103 unigram H ~7.44 nats; the 2.698 constant aligns with the OWT triangulation E, a different quantity)
c was fit at ep2 and extrapolated; deltas grow monotonically, so the ansatz systematically under-predicts how fast C* falls
ep3-8 resumed without optimizer state, adding noise (does not explain the full +0.85 gap)

beta_rep_sqrt_N.measured: Fitted β_rep ∝ N^-0.0843 (essentially flat/decreasing), not N^0.5

chinchilla_E_owt.hypothesis: Triangulate entropy floor E from 3 model sizes on OpenWebText
chinchilla_E_owt.pre_registered_band: PASS if 2.485 < E < 2.855 (E_WT103=2.68 lower-ish, GPT-2-medium OWT upper 2.855)
chinchilla_E_owt.verdict: PASS — E ≈ 2.49–2.53 nats, fit residuals ~0 nats

honest_summary: Of three pre-registered scaling claims, ONE (Chinchilla-E point estimate) passed; β_rep~√N failed its gate cleanly; the V²/T law is NOT SUPPORTED but its original 'falsification' was itself an invalid test — the corrected scan (slope -0.25) is the honest negative record.
